intro: Humboldtia krameriana, also known as Kramer's tree or Kramer's Humboldtia, is a plant species native to India, belonging to the family Sapindaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits, such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Humboldtia krameriana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
